steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The question asks what the text most strongly suggests about Demps's paintings. The passage states that Demps imitated Hair's fast painting technique, which produces the impressionistic qualities 'synonymous with the group's shared aesthetic.' McLendon, by contrast, painted with greater attention to detail — implying his work is less typical of the Highwaymen style.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• "Demps's reliance on the technique of fast painting likely accounts for his works being more aesthetically interesting than works by McLendon are.": The passage makes no judgment about aesthetic interest or quality — it only discusses technique and typicality. ✗
  • "Although it is evident that Demps adopted some of Hair's preferred techniques, Demps's works are less derivative of works by Hair than is typically acknowledged.": The passage says Demps imitated Hair's technique, suggesting his works are derivative, not less so. ✗
  • "The lack of precision with which they were executed suggests that they are inferior to works by either Hair or McLendon.": The passage never evaluates quality or ranks the artists — this is an unsupported value judgment. ✗
  • "Because of the manner in which they were created, they likely have visual qualities that are regarded as more typical of Florida Highwaymen paintings than the qualities in works by McLendon are.": Demps used the fast painting technique that defines the group's aesthetic; McLendon did not. This directly follows from the passage. ✓
That matches the correct answer — D is correct.
